audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for idol-food, knowledge puffing up, love building, one Divine/one Lord, conscience, liberty wounding weak sibling.

verses.restored: Now concerning things offered to idols: we know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds.
If anyone thinks to have known anything, one has not yet known as it is necessary to know.
But if anyone loves the Divine, this one has been known by him.
Therefore concerning eating things offered to idols, we know that an idol is nothing in the world, and that there is no Divine except one.
For even if there are so-called gods, whether in heaven or on earth, just as there are many gods and many lords,
yet for us there is one Divine, the Father, from whom are all things and we into him; and one Lord, Jesus Messiah, through whom are all things and we through him.
But not in all is this knowledge. Some, by habit until now toward the idol, eat as idol-offered, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.
Food will not present us to the Divine; neither if we do not eat do we lack, nor if we eat do we abound.
But watch lest somehow this authority/liberty of yours become a stumbling block to the weak.
For if anyone sees you, the one having knowledge, reclining in an idol temple, will not the conscience of the one being weak be built up into eating idol-offerings?
For the weak one is destroyed by your knowledge, the sibling because of whom Messiah died.
Thus sinning/misaligning against the siblings and striking their weak conscience, you sin/misalign against Messiah.
Therefore if food causes my sibling to stumble, I will certainly not eat flesh into the age, so that I may not cause my sibling to stumble.

translation_notes: Knowledge versus love is the governing contrast: accurate theology without love can still destroy a sibling.
Conscience is treated as a tender communal reality, not as mere ignorance to be mocked.
